controls.description: Formal access control policy and procedures must be established, documented, and disseminated to govern how access to systems and data is granted, managed, and revoked.
All user accounts must be formally requested, approved, provisioned, reviewed, and revoked through an automated, auditable lifecycle management process.
Systems must enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources using role-based and attribute-based access controls.
Information flows between system components, networks, and external systems must be controlled and restricted based on approved security policies.
Conflicting duties and areas of responsibility must be separated to reduce opportunities for unauthorized or unintended access or modification.

Users and service accounts granted minimum permissions required for assigned functions. Privileged Identity Management enforces JIT activation for all Azure AD directory roles and Azure RBAC privileged roles. Access reviews conducted quarterly for all privileged assignments.
Systems must enforce limits on consecutive failed logon attempts and automatically lock accounts after threshold is exceeded.
Systems must display an approved use notification banner before granting access, informing users of monitoring, acceptable use, and consent to terms.
Systems must limit the number of concurrent sessions allowed per user account to prevent unauthorized parallel access.
Systems must automatically lock after a defined period of inactivity and require re-authentication before resuming access.
Systems must automatically terminate sessions after a defined period of inactivity or upon user logout.
Systems must identify and document any actions permitted without identification or authentication, and restrict these to an approved minimum.
Remote access to GCC systems must be controlled through approved mechanisms with MFA, encryption, and continuous session monitoring.
Wireless access to GCC systems must be controlled through approved configurations with authentication, encryption, and monitoring.
Mobile devices accessing GCC systems must be enrolled in MDM, compliant with security policies, and able to be remotely wiped if lost or stolen.
Use of external information systems to access GCC resources must be authorized, controlled, and subject to security requirements equivalent to internal systems.
GCC systems must enforce controls that ensure information is shared only with authorized recipients based on data classification and need-to-know.
GCC systems must ensure that publicly accessible content does not include non-public information, and that publishing controls prevent unauthorized disclosure.
A comprehensive security awareness and training program must be established and delivered to all users on a recurring basis.
Security training must be tailored by role, with additional specialized modules for privileged users and administrators.
Security awareness training must be tailored by role, with specialized modules for privileged users, administrators, and developers.
Measures and validates compliance with AT-4 (Training Records) and its enhancements. Monitors CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with AU-1 (Audit and Accountability Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Defined audit events must be logged across all in-scope system components. Entra ID sign-in and audit logs, Azure Activity Log, resource diagnostic logs, and Defender alerts must all flow to the central Log Analytics workspace within 15 minutes.
Measures and validates compliance with AU-3 (Content of Audit Records) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with AU-4 (Audit Log Storage Capacity) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with AU-5 (Response to Audit Logging Process Failures) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Audit records reviewed and analyzed via Sentinel analytics rules and workbooks. Automated correlation of audit events to detect anomalies. Weekly audit review reports generated. Findings escalated to IR process within defined SLA.

Measures and validates compliance with AU-7 (Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with AU-8 (Time Stamps) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with AU-9 (Protection of Audit Information) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with AU-10 (Non-repudiation) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with AU-11 (Audit Record Retention) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
All security-relevant events must generate detailed audit records that are automatically collected and forwarded to a central secure logging platform.
Formal policy governing security assessments, system authorization, and continuous monitoring must be established and followed.
Security assessments and continuous monitoring must be performed to validate control effectiveness and detect posture changes in real time.
Measures and validates compliance with CA-3 (Information Exchange) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with CA-5 (Plan of Action and Milestones) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with CA-6 (Authorization) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
A documented continuous monitoring strategy must be fully implemented, including metrics, tools, and review processes.
Measures and validates compliance with CA-8 (Penetration Testing) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with CA-9 (Internal System Connections) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal configuration management policy and procedures must be established to control changes to systems and services throughout their lifecycle.
Approved secure baseline configurations must be identified, documented, and actively maintained for all system components and services.
All configuration changes must follow a formal, auditable change control process including approval, testing, and post-change validation.

Measures and validates compliance with CM-4 (Impact Analyses) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with CM-5 (Access Restrictions for Change) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
All systems must comply with approved secure configuration baselines with automated enforcement and drift detection.
Systems must be configured with only necessary functionality. Unneeded services, ports, protocols, and applications must be disabled or removed.
A complete, accurate, and automatically updated inventory of all system components must be maintained.

Measures and validates compliance with CM-9 (Configuration Management Plan) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with CM-10 (Software Usage Restrictions) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with CM-12 (Information Location) and its enhancements. Monitors AzurePolicy, AzureResourceGraph, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal contingency planning policies and procedures must be established to ensure system availability and rapid recovery during disruptions.
Measures and validates compliance with CP-2 (Contingency Plan) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with CP-3 (Contingency Training) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Tests the GCC High contingency plan to verify recovery procedures are effective and recovery objectives can be met. Conducts annual tabletop exercises, functional tests of Azure Site Recovery, and backup restoration tests to validate recovery capabilities.

Establishes and maintains an alternate storage site to store backup copies of information system data and software, ensuring geographic separation from the primary site and enabling recovery operations in the event of a primary site failure or disaster.
Measures and validates compliance with CP-7 (Alternate Processing Site) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with CP-8 (Telecommunications Services) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Critical data and system configurations must be backed up regularly with tested, verifiable recovery procedures.

Measures and validates compliance with CP-10 (System Recovery and Reconstitution) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal identification and authentication policy and procedures must be established and disseminated to govern how users and devices are identified and authenticated.
All users, devices, and services must be uniquely identified and authenticated using phishing-resistant MFA before accessing any GCC system or data.
All devices connecting to GCC systems must be uniquely identified and authenticated before access is granted.
User, device, and service identifiers must be uniquely assigned, managed through a formal process, and disabled when no longer active.
Authenticators (passwords, tokens, certificates, keys) must be issued, managed, rotated, and revoked through a formal, automated lifecycle process.
Systems must obscure authenticator feedback (e.g., passwords) during the authentication process to prevent shoulder surfing and information leakage.
GCC systems must use FIPS 140-2 or FIPS 140-3 validated cryptographic modules for all authentication operations.
Non-organizational users (contractors, partners, guests) accessing GCC systems must be uniquely identified and authenticated using approved methods.
GCC systems must require re-authentication when sessions exceed defined time limits or when sensitive operations are performed.
Users must undergo identity proofing at a level commensurate with the sensitivity of the systems and data they will access before accounts are provisioned.
Formal incident response policy and procedures must be established, documented, and communicated to all relevant personnel.
All personnel with incident response responsibilities must receive regular, role-specific training and participate in tabletop exercises.
The incident response capability must be regularly tested through simulations, tabletop exercises, and red-team activities.
Formal procedures for detecting, analyzing, and responding to security incidents must be established and followed.
Measures and validates compliance with IR-5 (Incident Monitoring) and its enhancements. Monitors MicrosoftDefenderXDR, AzureActivityLogs for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
All security incidents must be reported to appropriate stakeholders within defined timeframes with clear escalation paths.

Measures and validates compliance with IR-7 (Incident Response Assistance) and its enhancements. Monitors MicrosoftDefenderXDR, AzureActivityLogs for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
The incident response plan must be regularly tested and updated based on lessons learned from incidents and exercises.

Measures and validates compliance with MA-1 (System Maintenance Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MA-2 (Controlled Maintenance) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MA-3 (Maintenance Tools) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MA-5 (Maintenance Personnel) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MA-6 (Timely Maintenance) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureActivityLogs, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-1 (Media Protection Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-2 (Media Access) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-3 (Media Marking) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-4 (Media Storage) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-5 (Media Transport) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-6 (Media Sanitization) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with MP-7 (Media Use) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with PE-1 (Physical and Environmental Protection Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-2 (Physical Access Authorizations) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-3 (Physical Access Control) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-4 (Access Control for Transmission) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-5 (Access Control for Output Devices) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-6 (Monitoring Physical Access) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-8 (Visitor Access Records) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-9 (Power Equipment and Cabling) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-10 (Emergency Shutoff) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-11 (Emergency Power) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-12 (Emergency Lighting) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-13 (Fire Protection) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-14 (Environmental Controls) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-15 (Water Damage Protection) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-16 (Delivery and Removal) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PE-17 (Alternate Work Site) and its enhancements. Monitors Other for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal security planning policy and procedures must be developed, documented, and disseminated organization-wide.
A comprehensive System Security Plan (SSP) must be developed, maintained, and updated to accurately reflect the current security posture.
Measures and validates compliance with PL-4 (Rules of Behavior) and its enhancements. Monitors CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PL-10 (Baseline Selection) and its enhancements. Monitors CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PL-11 (Baseline Tailoring) and its enhancements. Monitors CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Personnel security policies and procedures must be established to screen, onboard, and manage personnel with system access.
All personnel requiring system access must undergo role-appropriate screening and background checks prior to access grant.
Access must be immediately revoked upon termination or role transfer, with automated processes for all accounts and systems.
Clear sanctions must be defined and consistently applied for violations of security policies by personnel.
Measures and validates compliance with PS-5 (Personnel Transfer) and its enhancements. Monitors EntraID, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PS-6 (Access Agreements) and its enhancements. Monitors EntraID, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PS-7 (External Personnel Security) and its enhancements. Monitors EntraID, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PS-8 (Personnel Sanctions) and its enhancements. Monitors EntraID, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with PS-9 (Position Descriptions) and its enhancements. Monitors EntraID, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with RA-1 (Risk Assessment Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with RA-2 (Security Categorization) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal risk assessments must be conducted regularly with documented mitigation plans for identified high and critical risks.
Systems must be scanned for vulnerabilities on a regular schedule with prioritized remediation based on risk severity.
All identified risks must have a formal response (mitigate, accept, transfer, avoid) with documented rationale and approval.
Measures and validates compliance with RA-9 (Criticality Analysis) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, AzureResourceGraph for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Formal policies and procedures for the acquisition of systems and services must ensure security requirements are addressed throughout the lifecycle.
Measures and validates compliance with SA-2 (Allocation of Resources) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with SA-3 (System Development Life Cycle) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Acquisition processes must incorporate security controls, including supply chain risk management and continuous monitoring of third-party services.
Complete, accurate, and up-to-date documentation for all systems and services must be maintained and readily available.

Measures and validates compliance with SA-8 (Security and Privacy Engineering Principles) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with SA-9 (External System Services) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with SA-10 (Developer Configuration Management) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with SA-11 (Developer Testing and Evaluation) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with SA-15 (Development Process, Standards, and Tools) and its enhancements. Monitors AzureResourceGraph, CustomApi for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Unsupported system components (hardware, software, firmware) must be identified, tracked, and replaced or mitigated in a timely manner.
Formal system and communications protection policy and procedures must be established, documented, and disseminated to govern all boundary and communications security controls.
System management functionality must be physically or logically separated from user functionality to prevent privilege escalation and unauthorized system modifications.
Shared system resources must prevent residual information from being accessible to unauthorized users after resource release or reallocation.
GCC systems must implement protections against denial-of-service (DoS) and distributed DoS (DDoS) attacks to maintain system availability.
GCC systems must monitor and control communications at all external and key internal boundaries using managed interfaces with deny-by-default posture.
All GCC data in transit must be protected using approved encryption protocols to ensure confidentiality and integrity.
GCC systems must terminate network connections after a defined period of inactivity or at the end of a session.
Cryptographic keys must be generated, distributed, stored, accessed, retired, and destroyed through a formal, auditable key management lifecycle.
GCC systems must implement FIPS-approved cryptographic algorithms and mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive information.
Collaborative computing devices and applications must provide explicit indication of use to users present in meeting spaces and prevent remote activation without consent.
GCC systems must define acceptable and unacceptable mobile code technologies, implement controls for their use, and prevent unauthorized mobile code execution.
GCC systems must implement secure DNS services with DNSSEC to prevent DNS spoofing, poisoning, and unauthorized name resolution.
All GCC data at rest must be encrypted using approved encryption algorithms to protect against unauthorized access to stored information.
GCC systems must implement process isolation to prevent processes from interfering with each other or accessing unauthorized memory spaces.

Measures and validates compliance with SI-1 (System and Information Integrity Policy and Procedures) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with SI-2 (Flaw Remediation) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, AzurePolicy for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Measures and validates compliance with SI-3 (Malicious Code Protection) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Continuous monitoring via Microsoft Sentinel with UEBA enabled. All Azure resource diagnostic logs forwarded to Log Analytics workspace. Alert rules active for anomalous sign-ins, privilege escalation, data exfiltration indicators, and lateral movement patterns.

Measures and validates compliance with SI-5 (Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Integrity of critical software, firmware, and data must be continuously monitored using cryptographic mechanisms to detect unauthorized modifications.
Measures and validates compliance with SI-8 (Spam Protection) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with SI-10 (Information Input Validation) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.

Measures and validates compliance with SI-12 (Information Management and Retention) and its enhancements. Monitors DefenderForEndpoint, MDE, AzureMonitorMetrics for evidence of control implementation and effectiveness.
Systems must implement modern memory protection technologies (DEP, ASLR, CFG) and prevent execution of unauthorized code.

pass_criteria.description: Access control policy is documented, reviewed annually, and actively enforced across all systems. All access decisions are traceable to policy.
100% of accounts are reviewed quarterly. Inactive accounts are disabled within 30 days. All account changes are logged and auditable.
All system resources enforce documented access authorizations. Zero unauthorized access attempts succeed. RBAC/ABAC policies are fully implemented.
All information flows are governed by documented policies. Unapproved flows are blocked and alerted within 5 minutes of detection.
Privileged role assignments are reviewed quarterly. No single account holds conflicting administrative roles. SoD violations trigger automated alerts.
100% of non-exempt accounts use phishing-resistant MFA and automated lifecycle management
Account lockout triggers after no more than 5 consecutive failed attempts. All lockout events are logged and alerted within 15 minutes.
All systems display the approved use notification banner before login. Banner content is reviewed annually and complies with agency policy.
Concurrent session limits are enforced for all privileged accounts. Session anomalies trigger alerts within 15 minutes.
All managed devices lock automatically after 15 minutes of inactivity. Screen lock requires re-authentication. Compliance rate is 100%.
Sessions are automatically terminated after 30 minutes of inactivity. All session termination events are logged.

policies.description: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication

pass_criteria.description: All unauthenticated actions are documented, approved, and limited to public-facing content only. No sensitive data is accessible without authentication.
100% of remote access sessions use MFA and encrypted connections. All remote sessions are logged and monitored for anomalies.
All wireless access points use WPA3 or equivalent encryption. Only approved devices may connect. Rogue access points are detected and blocked within 1 hour.
100% of mobile devices accessing GCC are enrolled in Intune, compliant with policy, and capable of remote wipe. Non-compliant devices are blocked.
Only approved external systems may access GCC resources. Conditional Access policies enforce compliance and MFA for all external system access.
Data sharing is controlled by classification labels and DLP policies. Unauthorized sharing attempts are blocked and alerted within 15 minutes.
All publicly accessible content is reviewed prior to publication. Automated scans detect sensitive data in public repositories and alert within 1 hour.
100% of users complete required security awareness training annually, with phishing simulation pass rate above 90%.
Privileged users receive specialized training modules with documented completion and assessment scores above 85%.
100% of users complete role-based security awareness training annually. Privileged users complete additional specialized modules with phishing simulation pass rate >95%.
Control implementation verified through evidence sources

Assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy is documented, reviewed annually, and actively enforced.
Continuous monitoring covers 100% of in-scope systems with daily assessment of critical controls. Posture deviations trigger automated alerts within 15 minutes.
Control implementation verified through evidence sources

Configuration management policy is documented, reviewed annually, and enforced through automated tools and processes.

policies.description: Secure Baseline Configuration Identification and Maintenance

pass_criteria.description: All production systems match approved secure baselines. Drift is detected and remediated within 24 hours.
100% of production configuration changes follow the approved change control process with testing and validation.
Configuration drift < 5% across all systems; approved baselines enforced
Configuration drift < 5% across all systems; approved baselines enforced
100% of systems comply with approved secure baselines. Any drift is detected and remediated within 24 hours.

policies.description: Least Functionality and Attack Surface Reduction

pass_criteria.description: Only approved services and ports are running on production systems. Unnecessary functionality is disabled or removed.
Complete asset inventory exists with <1% stale records and is updated automatically on any change.
Configuration drift < 5% across all systems; approved baselines enforced
Configuration drift < 5% across all systems; approved baselines enforced
Configuration drift < 5% across all systems; approved baselines enforced
Contingency planning policy is documented, reviewed annually, and integrated with incident response and disaster recovery procedures.
Control implementation verified through evidence sources
Control implementation verified through evidence sources

Annual contingency plan test conducted with documented test scenario, participants, and results. Azure Site Recovery test failover completed successfully with RTO validated. Backup restoration test verifies RPO compliance with data integrity confirmed. Test findings tracked in POAM with remediation within 90 days.

All critical system data and software must be stored at a geographically separate alternate site with GRS or GZRS replication enabled, replication lag under 15 minutes, and all backup vaults configured with cross-region restore capability.

Daily backups of critical data are performed with successful quarterly restore tests achieving Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) targets.

policies.description: Identification and Authentication Policy and Procedures

pass_criteria.description: I&A policy is documented, reviewed annually, and enforced through Entra ID and conditional access policies across all GCC systems.
100% of user accounts require phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2 or CBA). Legacy authentication is blocked. MFA coverage is verified daily.
100% of devices accessing GCC are Entra-joined or hybrid-joined with compliant device certificates. Unknown devices are blocked by Conditional Access.
All identifiers are unique and managed in Entra ID. Reuse of identifiers is prevented. Disabled identities are retained for 90 days for audit purposes.
Passwords meet complexity requirements and rotate per policy. Service account credentials rotate every 90 days. Compromised credentials are revoked within 1 hour.
All authentication interfaces obscure credentials during entry. No system displays entered authenticators in clear text. Verified through configuration compliance scans.
All cryptographic modules used for authentication are FIPS 140-2/3 validated. Non-compliant modules are blocked by policy and detected within 24 hours.
All non-organizational users access GCC through Entra B2B with MFA enforced. Guest accounts are reviewed quarterly and removed when no longer needed.
Sessions require re-authentication after 12 hours or after inactivity. Privileged operations require step-up MFA. Enforced via Conditional Access policies.
All users undergo IAL2+ identity proofing before account provisioning. Identity proofing records are maintained and auditable for 7 years.
Incident response policy is documented, reviewed annually, and disseminated to all incident response team members and stakeholders.
100% of incident response team members complete required training annually and participate in at least one tabletop exercise per year.
Incident response capability is tested at least annually with documented lessons learned and corrective actions tracked to closure.
All incidents are handled according to documented procedures with mean time to containment under 4 hours for critical incidents.
All incidents detected and escalated within defined SLA windows
Critical incidents are reported to leadership within 1 hour; all incidents are documented and escalated per policy.
All incidents detected and escalated within defined SLA windows

policies.description: System and Communications Protection Policy and Procedures

pass_criteria.description: System and communications protection policy is documented, reviewed annually, and enforced across all GCC boundary protection controls.

policies.description: Separation of System and User Functionality

pass_criteria.description: Administrative interfaces are segregated from user interfaces. Admin access requires dedicated privileged workstations or PAW. Verified through configuration compliance.
Shared resources are scrubbed upon release. No residual data is accessible after resource deallocation. Verified through configuration compliance scans quarterly.
Azure DDoS Protection Standard is enabled for all public endpoints. DoS attacks are detected within 5 minutes and mitigation activates automatically.
All external boundaries use deny-by-default firewall rules. Inbound and outbound traffic is inspected. Unauthorized connections are blocked and alerted within 5 minutes.
All data in transit uses TLS 1.2 or higher. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are disabled. Certificate validity is monitored and alerts fire 30 days before expiration.
Network connections are automatically terminated after 30 minutes of inactivity. VPN and remote sessions terminate after session end. Enforced via policy.
All cryptographic keys are stored in Azure Key Vault with HSM protection. Key rotation is automated per policy. Key access is logged and audited.
All encryption uses FIPS 140-2/3 approved algorithms (AES-256, RSA-2048+, SHA-256+). Non-compliant algorithms are blocked by policy and detected within 24 hours.
All collaboration devices provide visual/audio indicators when active. Remote activation is disabled by policy. Compliance verified through Intune configuration profiles.
Mobile code policies are enforced via Defender and Intune. Unauthorized script execution is blocked. Policy compliance is at 100% across all managed endpoints.
All GCC DNS zones use Azure DNS with DNSSEC enabled. DNS query logs are retained for 90 days. Anomalous DNS activity triggers alerts within 15 minutes.
100% of storage accounts, databases, and managed disks use AES-256 encryption at rest. Customer-managed keys are stored in Azure Key Vault HSM.
All containerized workloads run with process isolation enforced. Kernel-level protections (WDAC, AppLocker) are enabled on all GCC hosts. Compliance at 100%.
